text:
Abstract ABSTRAKTUR HUKUM PELINDUNGAN INVESTOR DI PASAR MODAL This research was initiated due to the fact that there has been regulatory changes in the Indonesian financial services sector. Many of the previous regulatory instruments that were introduced by the government institutions have been taken over by a new Financial Services Authority (Ojk) that replaced the supervision task handed over by the former regulatory body Bapepam-LK. The Bapepam-LK was established by the Ministry of Finance where it was supervised directly by the Minister. In contrast to its predecessor, Ojk is a new autonomous institution that directly reports to the House of Representatives (legislator) and the people. One of the main reasons why the Ojk was established by the legislator was the poor consumer protection in the financial services sector. This research is focused on how to exercise legal protection of investors in the capital market. The purpose of this research is to find out or identify who are the parties that can exercise their rights as protected by the law in the capital market. In this research the authors will analyse and interpret several regulations in the capital market, such as Law No. 8 of 1995 concerning the Capital Market and Law No. 21 of 2011 concerning the Financial Services Authority in order to reveal how to handle consumer complaints and maintain the efficiency of capital market.

Keywords: Legal Protection, Consumer Interests, Capital Market
